reviews.review_description:
UPDATE 2: I love what you've done. Such a massive improvement. I had goosebumps the whole time. Having more bullets and getting to play for a much longer time before dying has really turned this into a frightening and cool experience. It's like getting to be in a real life Stranger Things type world. What really stands out to me is the monsters you are creating, they are fantastic and unique from other games. Keep up the good work and keep adding things! Please! I am updating my review to five stars because you are clearly determined to make this amazing.

UPDATE: I wanted to respond to the developer. I know I can hit the skull with the gun, but I only knew that because someone else mentioned it in a review. The thing about hitting it with the gun that feels a little anticlimactic to me is everything in the world is slow mo, so it feels like I'm swatting at something underwater. A little boring. I get it if that's the kind of Upside Down world you were going for, which I'll say again is great. It has a HP Lovecraft vibe to me. I got to the next monster which was a larger skeleton. I think the issue is that I play other MR shooter games, mostly zombies coming into my home, and they're more like wave shooters, and they get the adrenaline pumping. There's a sense of action and urgency. I understand maybe that's not the world you wanted here, maybe you want it more of a slow burn, but people might get bored quickly. The reason I am so invested in giving feedback is because so far it's a great start and I wanna see it become a great, addicting game!

Thanks, guys, you've made a significant step forward in Mixed Reality! In my opinion, you've created the best Mixed Reality experience for today. Of course, it needs some improvement and polish, but what you've accomplished for now is already very impressive. I highly recommend to try it to all VR enthusiasts!
Great use of room mapping and entertaining gameplay. To the dev, given the steam frames limited MR capability, would it be possible to port this game to the frame? Thanks

developer_response.body: Thank you for the 5 star review, I'm really glad to hear when people like it! Here's a link if you want to send me a message beyond the game: https://www.realityrig.com/underlie-contact

reviews.review_description:
Underlie has a lot of potential, but is extremely hard to keep you alive for more than a few minutes, and easy to find some bugs with monsters pathfinding.

With some more polish this game could be awesome, and that's why I keep 5 star rating. Hope you keep working on this.

Edit: Just tried 1.65 update and it's good, but I still miss something that keeps me hooked for more than 10 minutes. Maybe an option to unlock an elevator after killing some monsters, to descent to new floors, would be a way to expand the game, and give to the player some sense of moving forward.

Underlie is an amazing experience, but it needs something more to keep players engaged. Hope you get to that point.

I've wanted to have an interior redisgn of my room for years and finally with this app, my room looks perfect. This profound and unusually excellent VR app has turned my room into the IKEA showcase that I've always wanted with pine green walls with white painted trim and wainscoting.

I bought the game on sale,
I really liked that it turns my house into hell, the design is very beautiful.

I have a hard time playing this game, I just can't do it.
I play and die within a minute, I can't progress, and it depresses me .

I'm not lowering the rating because I believe you will improve the game.
Please add difficulty levels and make a "very easy" mode for people like me who want to play for more than a minute.


Edit:
Hi, Sorry for the delay.... I gave the game another chance after the update. When I switched to the easier level I finally saw new and scary creatures :) Congratulations on the design, the design is one of the basic reasons I love the game.

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: Glad the "Less Difficult" mode helped you!

For readers: "Less Difficult" mode is actually an experimental progression system. The way it currently works, is If you don't get past a certain point in the game by approx 3 deaths, the game will prompt you if you want to turn it on. You can also just turn it on if you go to the "Options" on the main menu. I've tweaked it a bit since it v1.50 and it's still experimental--all these things could totally change!

If anybody wants to give me more feedback, please check out this page! https://www.realityrig.com/underlie-contact

reviews.review_description: So I got this like, a couple minutes ago and did a few testing, my sister and I, she ripped the headset off her head and almost flung it 😂😂. Well, thats how believable the meshes are. One thing though is that at a point, a skull was clipping with the mesh and was seemingly stuck, spent a few ammo on it but it didnt budge, alssooo, hold onnnn!!, did you make doors even when closed accessible to the monsters? Boy, I saw my door open and skulls came through lmao

reviews.review_title: A Glimpse of the future of gaming
reviews.review_description: Truly next-gen. Transform your room or whole home into a nightmare atmosphere, no cumbersome setup needed. Needs to be experienced to "get" just how revolutionary this is.

developer_response.body:
I really appreciate this comment.

For me, the "ultimate form of VR" is being able to actually walk around in a virtual world. Not thumbstick locomotion or arm swinging. I wanted this game to be revolutionary, and I'm so glad there's at least some people who see it that way!

I should mention, though, there is a form of setup: People still need to have their rooms scanned with the Quest room scanning (with any doorways between rooms properly in the scans).

reviews.review_title: I want to play more but.....
reviews.review_description: I really like this concept. This game turns my home into a dark dimension and I love it! I was able to scan most of my single-story home- all the rooms I wanted to play in, but I ended up playing in just one small room the entire time. There isn't a tutorial for how the weapon operates so that took me a while to figure out how it charges and saves ammo. The game is a bit too hard to stay alive long enough to enjoy the game. When I'm out of ammo, I tried to hit the skulls with the gun but I seem to still take health damage. The game starts out with a story narration but I don't understand how it ties into the game. It was the comment, "do you see anything yet?" that made me realize I should walk around. Again, there is nothing in the game that explains how to play. You just jump right in and have to figure it out. You'll just die a lot until you figure it out. I love how this game looks and am rating this based on its potential. Please continue to develop. Update your website and add social media links, especially Discord for players and potential players to discuss and stay up-to-date with updates. Great job!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the 5 star review, and for the video you made!

That's a bummer you played in only one room when you wanted more. I've since changed the game menus to try to help people not skip past adding additional rooms. (Maybe when you played, you scanned rooms but didn't 'select/checkmark' the rooms you wanted)

The first time that you play in a session, it does some mesh generation stuff. You can walk around while that's happening. A flash of light reveals the room meshes when the generation is complete.

I've also added a 'damage' lighting effect to help players understand better where they get hit from!

reviews.review_description: woth it for sure!!!  most definitely headed in the right direction the graphics is unreal wish it had a couple more monsters or different guns or even spells that you can cast dont take my idea lol.   Great game thanks

Interesting concept but after I emptied the few shots my gun held i found myself swatting at flying skills until I died.. app manager reached  out o me about refilling ammo i will try again Update, tried again found ammo containers but enemies seemed to stop spawning. Waited  several minutes and nothing happened.

developer_response.body:
I updated the game more today. There's been a couple changes since v1.43 based on what you said.

When the enemies stopped spawning for you, maybe a "portal" was ready to open. It's confused me in the past, and I had added a sound effect to try to make people aware something was there, but it wasn't enough really. The update today makes an ammo canister spawn in front of your doorway after a delay IF the player hadn't gotten close enough to the portal doorway for it to open within about 20 seconds of it getting active. I hope that can help lead people to seeing it open. (I realize now I should also add a sound cue when the opening actually happens, but I haven't done that yet)

Also, in many cases now there will be a canister that spawns near the player's feet on the first play of a session, along with a dialogue line. Hopefully that will help people realize about the ammo.

Thanks for your feedback!

reviews.review_description: At the time of this writing, this is really one of a kind. Loving it!

reviews.review_title: Your home became grotesque and scary
reviews.review_description: Interesting. Some problems with the transformation of the environments, which occasionally return real, but I found that it is not the developer's fault but a mix of my mistakes and limitations of my headset.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the positive rating!


I have a some guesses what you might have seen.


First thing to know is that the game uses the Quest 3's "Depth API" to use the estimated depth of the "real world." This is for blending the passthrough and the virtual world. If something in the real world is close enough to you, and significantly in front of the generated mesh in most cases it should show up "in front of" the virtual world. So one possibility is that the generated mesh wasn't "in front of" things in the real world in the area you were looking.

Note: you can actually turn the depth-based blending off if you pause the game and go to the Options menu.


Something to note is that even if the game world is dark, the Quest 3 needs light for tracking. (Maybe the Quest 3S doesn't?) So another possibility is some kind of glitch in the Quest 3's tracking or depth sensing. Also, if something in the game itself is dark without lights on it, the "real world" passthrough might be visible there.

Another thing is that the game is only meant for playing on one "floor" of a home. I tested going up a staircase but the tracking would glitch. So you might be experiencing that some kind of tracking glitch like that, especially if you're going to a room on a different level.

If you want to show me a video, you could upload the video somewhere and send me a link here:


https://www.realityrig.com/underlie-contact

reviews.review_description: Starting the game stacks on "scan" and nothing goes on as well

developer_response.body:
I realized you might have clicked "Don't allow" when there was a system pop-up asking  to let the game use "spatial data."

I've updated the game. Now, if somebody clicks that, the game should show a message telling them what they can do to enable it.

I'd like to try to get this working for you! You can message me here:  https://www.realityrig.com/underlie-contact
